package.brief: HH the Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday. The meeting reviewed bilateral relations and ways to expand cooperation, with a focus on economic ties. Regional developments in the Middle East were also discussed. The visit underscores the strong diplomatic ties between Qatar and Pakistan.

article.headline: HH the Amir Receives Prime Minister of Pakistan at Amiri Diwan
article.body:
**Doha, Qatar – 7 June 2026** – HH the Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday.

During the meeting, the two leaders reviewed the strong bilateral relations between Qatar and Pakistan and discussed ways to enhance cooperation across various fields, according to a statement from the Amiri Diwan.

Officials said the talks focused on expanding economic and trade partnerships, with both sides expressing a shared commitment to deepening investment ties.

In remarks during the meeting, HH the Amir said: "We value Pakistan's stance and look forward to deeper economic cooperation between our two countries."

The discussions also covered regional developments in the Middle East, with an emphasis on promoting peace and stability in the region, the statement added.

The visit of the Pakistani Prime Minister reflects the enduring friendship and strategic partnership between the two nations, which have maintained close diplomatic relations for decades.

Qatar and Pakistan have historically cooperated on multiple fronts, including energy, trade, and labour, and this meeting is expected to further strengthen those ties.

The meeting was attended by senior officials from both sides, though further details of the discussions were not disclosed.

This engagement is part of Qatar's ongoing efforts to bolster bilateral relations with key Asian partners and contribute to regional dialogue.

package.instagram_caption: HH the Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday. The leaders reviewed bilateral relations and discussed expanding economic cooperation. #Qatar #Pakistan #Diplomacy #AmiriDiwan #QatarPakistanRelations #Leadership

package.brief: Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art has opened a new exhibition titled 'Resolutions' to mark its 15th anniversary. The exhibition explores the museum's foundation through four key areas: its origins under Sheikh Hassan bin Mohammed bin Ali Al Thani, its exhibition history, its engagement with knowledge circulation in the Arab world, and its educational programmes. Entry is free for all visitors, underscoring Mathaf's commitment to accessibility and cultural enrichment.

article.headline: Mathaf Opens 'Resolutions' Exhibition Celebrating 15 Years of Arab Modern Art
article.body:
Doha, Qatar – Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art has inaugurated a new exhibition, 'Resolutions', commemorating 15 years since the museum's founding. The exhibition, which opened to the public on 10 June 2026, offers a reflective journey through the institution's history and its role in the Arab art landscape.

According to officials, 'Resolutions' examines Mathaf's foundation through four defining areas. These include the museum's origins under the patronage of Sheikh Hassan bin Mohammed bin Ali Al Thani, its exhibition history, its engagement with the circulation of knowledge in the Arab world, and its educational programmes.

The exhibition traces the evolution of Mathaf from a private collection into a leading public museum dedicated to modern and contemporary Arab art. It highlights key milestones and the vision that has guided the institution over the past decade and a half.

'Resolutions' also underscores Mathaf's commitment to fostering dialogue and learning. The educational programmes featured in the exhibition showcase the museum's efforts to engage diverse audiences, from students to art enthusiasts, in understanding Arab modernism.

Entry to the exhibition is free for all visitors, in line with Mathaf's mission to make art accessible to the community. The museum is open daily, with extended hours on weekends.

The 15th-anniversary exhibition serves as a testament to Qatar's growing cultural sector and its support for the arts. Mathaf continues to play a pivotal role in preserving and promoting Arab artistic heritage, contributing to the nation's cultural identity.

'Resolutions' will run until 31 December 2026, offering ample opportunity for residents and visitors to explore the rich history of Arab modern art through the lens of Mathaf's journey.

package.instagram_caption: Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art opens 'Resolutions' – a special exhibition celebrating 15 years of Arab modern art. Explore the museum's origins under Sheikh Hassan bin Mohammed bin Ali Al Thani, its exhibition history, and educational programmes. Free entry for all. #Mathaf #ArabModernArt #QatarCulture #DohaMuseums #ArtExhibition #15YearsOfMathaf

package.brief: The Ministry of Public Health (MoPH) has launched a summer mosquito-control programme deploying drones and biological fish-based larvicide across 18 districts, coordinated with the Ministry of Municipality and running through October. The initiative aims to reduce mosquito-borne diseases during peak season, with officials emphasising shared public-health responsibility.

article.headline: Ministry of Public Health launches summer mosquito-control programme across 18 districts
article.body:
The Ministry of Public Health (MoPH) has announced the launch of a comprehensive summer mosquito-control programme, deploying drones and biological fish-based larvicide across 18 districts. The initiative, coordinated with the Ministry of Municipality, will run through October, according to officials.

The programme targets areas with high mosquito activity during the summer months, aiming to reduce the risk of mosquito-borne diseases. The MoPH Director of Communicable Diseases stated that public-health vigilance during summer is a shared responsibility.

Drones will be used to survey and treat hard-to-reach breeding sites, while biological larvicide—using fish that feed on mosquito larvae—offers an environmentally friendly control method. The approach aligns with global best practices for integrated vector management.

The MoPH urged residents to support the efforts by eliminating standing water around homes and reporting potential breeding sites. The Ministry of Municipality will assist with ground-level inspections and community outreach.

This year's programme expands on previous campaigns, covering more districts and incorporating advanced technology. Officials noted that early and sustained intervention is key to preventing outbreaks of diseases such as dengue fever and Rift Valley fever.

Residents are encouraged to follow preventive measures, including using insect repellent and wearing long sleeves during dawn and dusk. The MoPH will provide regular updates on the programme's progress.

The initiative reflects Qatar's commitment to public health and environmental sustainability, leveraging innovation to protect the community during the summer season.

package.instagram_caption: The Ministry of Public Health has launched its summer mosquito-control programme, using drones and biological fish-based larvicide across 18 districts. The initiative runs through October in coordination with the Ministry of Municipality. Let's do our part by eliminating standing water and reporting breeding sites. #Qatar #PublicHealth #MosquitoControl #SummerSafety #MoPH #QatarLiving
package.story_copy: MoPH launches summer mosquito-control programme using drones and biological larvicide across 18 districts. The initiative runs through October.

package.brief: The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 concluded on 24 May 2026, and editorial operations are updating its status from scheduled to completed. The expo brought together education leaders and stakeholders to discuss advancements in the sector. This administrative update ensures accurate event lifecycle management on Qatar Living.

article.headline: Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 Concludes, Status Updated to Completed
article.body:
The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026, a key gathering for education stakeholders, concluded on 24 May 2026. The event, which brought together leaders and experts in the field, focused on advancing educational practices and fostering collaboration within the sector.

According to editorial operations at Qatar Living, the expo's status is being moved from EventScheduled to Completed. This update ensures that the event listing accurately reflects its lifecycle and provides clarity for users browsing past events.

The expo served as a platform for discussions on educational leadership, innovation, and policy development. Participants included representatives from various educational institutions and government bodies, highlighting the importance of continuous improvement in Qatar's education landscape.

As Qatar continues to prioritise education as a cornerstone of its national development, events like the Qatar Education Leadership Expo play a vital role in shaping the future of learning. The successful conclusion of the 2026 edition marks another milestone in the country's commitment to educational excellence.

For more information on past and upcoming events, visit the Qatar Living events page.

package.instagram_caption: The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 has successfully concluded, marking a significant step forward in educational collaboration and innovation. Stay tuned for more updates on future events shaping Qatar's education landscape. #QatarEducation #EducationLeadership #QatarEvents #QatarLiving #EducationExpo #Qatar2026

package.brief: Qatar Airways announced it will resume direct flights to Helsinki, Finland, and Tokyo Haneda, Japan, starting 15 July 2026. The move expands the airline's global network to over 160 destinations this summer, reinforcing Qatar's position as a key aviation hub. The routes cater to both leisure and business travellers, with increased frequency during peak season. This development underscores Qatar Airways' strategic focus on connectivity and network growth.

article.headline: Qatar Airways to Resume Direct Flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda from July
article.body:
Qatar Airways has announced the resumption of direct flights to Helsinki, Finland, and Tokyo Haneda, Japan, effective 15 July 2026, according to a statement from the airline. The move further expands the carrier's global network, which will reach over 160 destinations this summer.

The Helsinki route will operate four times weekly from 15 July, increasing to daily services from 1 August. Tokyo Haneda will receive four weekly flights from 15 July, complementing the existing daily service to Tokyo Narita. The new frequencies are designed to meet growing demand for travel between Doha and these key markets.

“We are pleased to restore connectivity with Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda — two strategically important markets for our network,” said Qatar Airways Chief Commercial Officer in a statement. “This expansion reflects our commitment to providing passengers with more choices and seamless connectivity.”

The resumption of flights to Helsinki marks the return of a vital link between Qatar and Finland, supporting trade, tourism, and cultural exchange. Similarly, the addition of Tokyo Haneda offers travellers greater flexibility and access to central Tokyo, enhancing the travel experience.

Qatar Airways' network expansion is part of its broader strategy to strengthen its position as a leading global airline. The airline continues to invest in new routes and increased frequencies, leveraging Doha's strategic location as a hub connecting East and West.

Passengers can book flights through the Qatar Airways website or via travel agents. The airline advises travellers to check entry requirements for their destinations before travel.

package.instagram_caption: Qatar Airways is expanding its network! ✈️ From 15 July, we're resuming direct flights to Helsinki, Finland, and Tokyo Haneda, Japan, bringing our global destinations to over 160 this summer. Whether you're heading to the Nordic capital or the heart of Tokyo, we've got you covered. Book now! #QatarAirways #Helsinki #TokyoHaneda #Aviation #Travel #NetworkExpansion
